_meta.description: Georgia Social Studies standards mapping for Atlas topics
_meta.note: Georgia organizes SS standards by domain. Grade 6 = Latin America/Canada/Europe/Australia, Grade 7 = Africa/SW Asia/S/SE Asia, Grade 8 = Georgia Studies, HS = US History/World History/Govt/Economics.

Ancient Civilizations (Mesopotamia, Egyp.description: Describe the impact of European exploration and colonization on Latin America and the Caribbean

Ancient Greece & Rome.description: Analyze the rise of classical civilizations and their lasting impact on Europe

Geography Skills & Map Reading.description: Locate and describe major physical features, countries, and regions on maps

Intro to World Religions.description: Describe the origins and beliefs of major world religions and their cultural impact

Early Chinese & Indian Civilizations.description: Analyze contributions of ancient Asian civilizations to world development

Government & Citizenship (Intro).description: Compare and contrast various forms of government in Europe, Latin America, and other regions

Medieval World & Renaissance.description: Analyze continuity and change in Africa, Southwest Asia, and Southern/Eastern Asia

Age of Exploration.description: Analyze the impact of European exploration and colonization on Africa and Asia

World Geography & Cultures.description: Locate and describe physical and human features of Africa, Southwest Asia, and Asia

Enlightenment & Revolution.description: Analyze change and conflict in modern Africa, Southwest Asia, and Asia

Africa, Asia & the Americas.description: Analyze the impact of colonialism and independence movements across regions

Economics (Supply & Demand).description: Compare economic systems; analyze trade and voluntary exchange in Africa, SW Asia, and Asia

U.S. Constitution & Government.description: Analyze the impact of the Constitutional Convention and the formation of the U.S. government

Civil War & Reconstruction.description: Analyze the causes, events, and effects of the Civil War and Reconstruction in Georgia

Industrialization & Immigration.description: Evaluate key political, social, and economic developments in Georgia from 1877 to 1918

World War I & II.description: Analyze Georgia's role in WWI, the Great Depression, and WWII

Geography & Map Skills.description: Describe Georgia's geography and the impact of location on the state's development

Economics & Trade.description: Analyze the role of economic factors in Georgia's development and growth

U.S. Government & Politics.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the political philosophies that shaped the U.S. government

The Constitution in Practice.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the U.S. Constitution — structure, amendments, and application

Civil Rights & Civil Liberties.description: Analyze the meaning and importance of each of the rights guaranteed by the Bill of Rights

State & Local Government.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the operation of Georgia's state and local government

Political Parties & Elections.description: Explain the role of political parties, interest groups, and media in American politics

The Judicial System.description: Describe the structure and function of the judicial branch at federal and state levels

World History: Ancient to Modern.description: Analyze the origins and impact of the earliest civilizations on world history

Imperialism & Colonialism.description: Describe the impact of industrialization and imperialism on the modern world

World Wars & Cold War.description: Identify the causes, events, and results of the World Wars and the Cold War

Globalization & Modern Conflicts.description: Analyze the end of the Cold War and emergence of modern global challenges

Human Rights & International Law.description: Evaluate the development of human rights movements and international organizations

Comparative Government.description: Analyze the ideologies that have shaped modern political systems globally

U.S. History (Comprehensive).description: Compare and contrast the development of English colonial regions and their impact

Manifest Destiny & Expansion.description: Explain the process of economic growth, westward expansion, and sectional conflict

The Progressive Era.description: Analyze efforts to reform American society and politics in the Progressive Era

The Great Depression & New Deal.description: Analyze the causes and consequences of the Great Depression and the New Deal

Cold War & Vietnam.description: Identify the origins and major events of the Cold War including the Vietnam era

Modern America (1980–Present).description: Analyze political, economic, and social changes in modern America

Microeconomics & Macroeconomics.description: Explain fundamental economic concepts: scarcity, opportunity cost, supply and demand

International Relations.description: Explain the benefits and costs of international trade and foreign exchange

Comparative Political Systems.description: Demonstrate knowledge of how differing political systems operate globally

Contemporary Global Issues.description: Analyze current issues and the role of government in addressing economic challenges

Personal Finance & Economic Policy.description: Apply economic principles to personal financial decisions: budgeting, saving, investing

AP Government & Politics Review.description: Comprehensive review of American government and political institutions (AP level)
